source: SafePro: Evaluating the Safety of Professional-Level AI Agents

official_github.relevant_file_correspondence: The dataset and four evaluation files are byte-identical at paper-time and acquisition commits. Later commits only alter README/project metadata in the inspected SafePro-specific surface.
official_github.license: The inherited repository root LICENSE places content outside enterprise/ under MIT. It does not document task-author authority over GDPval-derived prompts/reference files or provide a dataset-specific provenance/license manifest.

official_hugging_face.correspondence: The Hugging Face JSON is byte-identical to both pinned GitHub copies. Hugging Face postdates immutable v2 by about six hours and has no license tag.

anomalies: One extra Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services row has risk_category 'Discrimination/bias' rather than the paper's 'Discrimination / bias'; removing it reconciles both paper sector and risk totals.
The extra row has task_id 4122f866-01fa-400b-904d-fa171cdfasdk and is not identified as excluded by the released runner.
The public JSON contains 204 non-null original prompt fields, not the paper's 195 transformed-task count, and has no source/transformation indicator.
208 reference URLs point to the mutable openai/gdpval main branch; reference bytes and pinned upstream revision are absent.
The released runner depends on absolute author-local dataset and reference-file paths and installs unpinned packages at runtime.
No paper output rows, trajectories, judgments, result tables, random sample IDs, or aggregation reproduction artifacts are released.

audit_scope: Full immutable paper and appendix read; all 276 public dataset records structurally inspected; complete recursive Git trees and commit metadata inspected; all SafePro-specific runner/scorer/data/README/license files preserved at paper-time and acquisition commits. The full inherited 3,149-file OpenHands fork was manifest-audited rather than duplicated. No model APIs were rerun.
